ACCESS-Consortium: The ACCESS Consortium brings together Australian institutions contributing to the Australian Community Climate and Earth System Simulator (ACCESS) and its submissions to the Coupled Model Intercomparison Project (CMIP). Participating organisations contribute to different ACCESS models and scientific activities, supported through a combination of institutional investment and Australian Government research infrastructure and research programs, together with international collaborations.
AWI: Alfred Wegener Institute for Polar and Marine Research
CNRM-CERFACS: CNRM (Centre National de Recherches Meteorologiques, Toulouse 31057, France), CERFACS (Centre Europeen de Recherche et de Formation Avancee en Calcul Scientifique, Toulouse 31057, France)
KIOST: A consortium of Korean research institutions contributing to CMIP7 through Earth system modeling. Led by the Korea Institute of Ocean Science and Technology (KIOST) in collaboration with Hanyang University (HYU).
MRI: MRI (Meteorological Research Institute) conducts research for the improvement of information issued by JMA (Japan Meteorological Agency) on weather, earthquakes, volcanic activity, climatic conditions and other phenomena.
NCAR: The National Science Foundation National Center for Atmospheric Research (NSF NCAR) manages the development and maintenance of the Community Earth System Model (CESM).
NOAA-GFDL: US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Laboratory.
SYSU: Sun Yat-sen University, a public university in Guangzhou, China.
UKNCSP: UKNCSP is a strategic partnership between the Met Office and six NERC-funded research centres in the UK. This partnership is consist of British Antarctic Survey(BAS), British Geological Survey(BGS), Met Office, National Centre for Atmospheric Science (NCAS), National Centre for Earth Observation(NCEO), National Oceanography Centre (NOC), UK Centre for Ecology & Hydrology (UKCEH) with a mission to unite and harness UK climate science capability, advance international partnerships, and drive solutions for a resilient, net-zero world.
dlr: The German Aerospace Center DLR is the Federal Republic of Germany's research centre for aviation, space, energy, transport, and security. The DLR Institute of Atmospheric Physics conducts research into the physics and chemistry of the global atmosphere, from the ground up to the upper edge of the middle atmosphere at an altitude of approximately 120 km.
